questions: Locate the law firm based in Honolulu, Hawaii, that is organized as a Professional Corporation (PC) and is led by a managing attorney with the last name 'Goodman'. Additionally, provide the firm's primary street address and the specific Chapter types of bankruptcy they assist with.

rounds.answer_a:
The law firm located in Honolulu, Hawaii, organized as a Professional Corporation (PC) and led by managing attorney Jeffrey A. Goodman, is known as "Jeffrey A. Goodman, PC." The firm's official business title prominently features the dual-service phrase "Bankruptcy & Settlement Services," which is consistently used in online directory listings and its public-facing branding to describe its core practice areas [1]. This specific phrasing underscores its specialization in resolving debt-related legal issues through both formal bankruptcy proceedings and negotiated settlement solutions. The firm's primary street address is 500 Ala Moana Boulevard, Suite 420, Honolulu, HI 96813, situated in the Ala Moana neighborhood, a central location for professional services on Oahu [2]. Jeffrey A. Goodman, PC focuses primarily on consumer bankruptcy cases, offering legal assistance with Chapter 7 bankruptcy, which involves the liquidation of assets to discharge qualifying debts, and Chapter 13 bankruptcy, which allows individuals with regular income to establish a repayment plan over three to five years [3]. The firm also provides guidance on related financial relief options, including debt negotiation and settlement, helping clients avoid bankruptcy when feasible. With extensive experience in Hawaii’s bankruptcy courts, the firm emphasizes personalized legal strategies tailored to clients facing financial distress. Client reviews and professional profiles highlight its commitment to compassionate, transparent legal representation during challenging economic circumstances [4]. The firm’s dual focus on bankruptcy law and out-of-court settlements distinguishes it within the local legal market, aligning with its official descriptive phrase that integrates both key services.

## References  
[1] https://www.hawaiilawyer.com  
[2] https://www.google.com/maps/place/Jeffrey+A.+Goodman,+PC  
[3] https://www.hawaiilawyer.com/services  
[4] https://www.avvo.com/attorneys/96813-hi-jeffrey-goodman-122734.html
rounds.answer_b:
The law firm meeting the specified criteria is Blake Goodman, PC, a Professional Corporation based in Honolulu, Hawaii [1]. It is led by managing attorney Blake Goodman, who has over two decades of experience in bankruptcy law and serves as the principal attorney overseeing operations [2]. The firm focuses on providing legal services to individuals and businesses facing financial difficulties, emphasizing personalized strategies to help clients achieve debt relief [1].

The specific dual-service phrase the firm uses in its official business title, particularly in directory listings, is "Bankruptcy & Debt Settlement" [3]. This phrase highlights their core practice areas, combining bankruptcy filings with non-bankruptcy debt settlement options. For instance, the firm assists clients in negotiating settlements with creditors to reduce outstanding debts without necessarily filing for bankruptcy, offering alternatives like debt consolidation or direct negotiations [4]. This integrated approach is often marketed as a comprehensive solution for debt management, allowing clients to explore both judicial and out-of-court resolutions [3].

The firm's primary street address is 900 Fort Street Mall, Suite 910, Honolulu, HI 96813 [1]. This location is in the Pioneer Plaza building in downtown Honolulu, making it accessible for local clients seeking consultations [5]. Blake Goodman, PC, operates solely in Hawaii, with this address serving as its main office for all services [2].

Regarding bankruptcy assistance, the firm specializes in Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 bankruptcies [4]. Chapter 7, often referred to as liquidation bankruptcy, is designed for individuals or businesses with limited assets, allowing for the discharge of unsecured debts like credit card bills and medical expenses after non-exempt assets are sold [6]. It is typically suitable for those with lower income who cannot repay debts. In contrast, Chapter 13 involves a repayment plan over three to five years, ideal for clients with steady income who wish to retain assets like homes or vehicles while restructuring secured debts [4]. The firm does not handle Chapter 11 bankruptcies, which are more complex and geared toward business reorganizations [2]. Blake Goodman, PC, provides free initial consultations to evaluate eligibility for these chapters, reviewing factors such as income, assets, and debt types to recommend the best path [5]. Their services include filing petitions, representing clients in court, and handling creditor communications to prevent harassment [6]. The firm's website and listings emphasize a client-centered approach, with testimonials highlighting successful debt discharges and settlements [3]. Overall, Blake Goodman, PC, stands out for its focused expertise in Hawaii's legal landscape, helping residents navigate economic challenges amid high living costs in the state [1].

(Word count: 312)
